reviews.review_msg: Inigo is an amazing prof and is so passionate about what he teaches. I dont understand all the hate he recieves, hes an easy marker and the content is very interesting. The only issue I had was he spoke quietly so you really have to listen. He also posts recorded lectures from covid if you need to catch up. I really recommend him as a prof!!

Take it if you want it to ruin your GPA. Each exam comes from roughly 80 pages of lecture, but he only puts questions from 4 pages only, so if you are not confident in that random section then you will not do good. And he doesn't curve even though he said if the average is under B- he will.
He's not necessarily bad, just so monotone and super unclear grading criteria. Just don't take it if you don't need to. I cried throughout the entire semester. Nice guy though

Course content wasn't necessarily hard (especially if you have taken other UD BISC courses), the testing was brutal. Few questions and few things tested on, maybe 5 out of the 70 slides applicable to the exam, and sometimes multiple questions on a single topic. Attend lectures as he will give similar examples to questions on the exams.

Inigo is the worst biology professor I've had. The course material isn't hard, but he makes it confusing, which shows in the low class averages. His lectures are monotone and hard to follow. There are no learning objectives to guide you, and his slides are useless since they don't appear on the exams.

Lectures are incredibly boring, monotone and largely uninformative. Too many random figures or wall of text with little detail forcing you to learn by just listening. No practice questions and his testing is bizarre. Online testing worth very few marks that focuses on niche topics, not the important ones. His class averages speak for themselves.

Did not explain concepts in great depth, seeming more focused on dragging himself through slides than prioritizing student learning/interest. No practise problems performed/provided for the physics portion. Exams fairly graded but covered an extremely small percentage of the topics discussed in lecture and skipped over main themes of the course.

The lecture slides had too many words and pictures with no real descriptions to study off from. The verbal lecture itself was slow and dreary but if you watch the lecture diligently and really STUDY & understand what he is saying you will do good on exams. Exams really tested whether or not you LISTENED to the lecture. Lecture slides do not help

Lectures are the most boring I've ever experienced from a class. Nothing but multiple small figures on each slide, with few (if any) words or explanations. Spends 5-10mins talking about the figures in each slide, says important topics/terms that aren't written anywhere so you have to guess what he's saying. So much unimportant physics and details.

Absolutely hate him, with a passion! His exams are out of 20 marks, for like 12 questions. The concepts aren't too difficult, but he gives you little to NO ROOM to show your understanding. 100% DO NOT RECOMMEND! Literally punching air for taking this class with him in my last sem at SFU. Absolutely dreadful!!!!
